title: Using RL Agent to Detect and Remediate ETL Pipeline Failures - Anna Marie Benzon
summary: An RL agent with deterministic anomaly detection automates ETL pipeline failure diagnosis and remediation. The system detects schema drift, null-rate spikes, type changes, and runtime failures, then selects repair actions like retry, schema coercion, rollback, quarantine, or escalation, with safety guardrails ensuring bounded remediation.
writeup: This talk presents a reinforcement learning agent that detects and remediates ETL pipeline failures, covering schema drift, null-rate spikes, type changes, and runtime failures. The agent relies on interpretable Q-learning with a bounded action set: retry, schema coercion, rollback, quarantine, and escalation, paired with an external safety layer that constrains its choices. The interpretability and bounded actions matter because data pipeline remediation carries real risk, and keeping actions auditable and constrained limits the damage from a wrong decision. Evaluation across 30 synthetic runs reported minutes-scale recovery for the cases that were resolved.
